Nagwa Ibrahim is a Web and Graphics Designer with contact information including email, phone number, GitHub, LinkedIn, and a personal website.

Nagwa Ibrahim obtained a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Cairo University in September 2015, and previously completed a Higher Secondary Certificate in May 2011 and a Secondary School Certificate in June 2009 from the Egyptian Ministry of Education.
From September 2015 to August 2017, Nagwa Ibrahim worked as a full-time Web and Graphics Designer at Virtuous Media Point, where she designed websites and graphic assets using various software.

From January 2014 to August 2015, Nagwa Ibrahim completed an internship as a Web Designing and Development Intern at e-sense IT Solutions Pvt. Ltd., where she designed website solutions, gathered requirements, and participated in the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC).

Nagwa Ibrahim developed several websites during her education, including www.nitka.com, Nagpur United Corporation (admin), Mintmetrix.com, Tagline Videos (admin), Smartbadge (admin), www.shreekiaspack.co.in, www.3staragroproducts.com, and www.luckystationery.co.in, utilizing technologies like HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and jQuery.

Nagwa Ibrahim designed a wide range of graphic materials, including Logos, Brochures, Advertising Banners, Visiting Cards, Pamphlets, and Hoardings, demonstrating strong creative and technical skills.
A web-based disease diagnosis expert system was developed as a final year project, offering online doctor consultations and health suggestions for various diseases using web technologies.
A Shopping Management System was developed using C++.
The individual possesses basic skills in C, JAVA, and C++ programming languages, along with web technologies like HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and jQuery.
The individual is proficient in graphic design tools such as Corel Draw, Adobe Photoshop, and Adobe Illustrator, and has experience with MySQL 5.0 database and IDEs like Sublime Text and Notepad++.
The individual is familiar with operating systems Windows XP and Windows 7.
In 2015, the individual coordinated a project competition, FACE-IT, at Cairo University, managing participants and event logistics.
In 2015, the individual presented a project at the "Innovesta ’15" competition held at the Faculty of Engineering, Ain Shams University, Cairo, Egypt.
In 2015, the individual delivered a national-level paper presentation at "Tech-Wave 2015" held at Future University in Egypt, New Cairo, Egypt, sharing insights and research findings.

Egypt. A land steeped in millennia, a cradle of civilization now pulsing with modern ambition. She is a nation defined by the lifeblood of the Nile, a silver ribbon carving through ochre deserts and nourishing fertile lands. Ancient echoes resonate from the pyramids of Giza, silent sentinels guarding secrets of pharaohs and gods, while the bustling metropolis of Cairo, a vibrant heart, beats with the energy of over a hundred million people. 

Egypt is a place where tradition and innovation collide. A nation actively shaping its future, as evidenced by the rising generation of engineers like Nagwa Ibrahim – a Cairo University graduate, honed by the Egyptian Ministry of Education’s rigorous standards, and eager to contribute to a technologically advancing society. Her participation in competitions like “Innovesta ’15” at Ain Shams University and national presentations at “Tech-Wave 2015” at Future University in New Cairo demonstrate a commitment to pushing boundaries and sharing knowledge. 

She is a country of contrasts: the timeless majesty of ancient monuments alongside the dynamic growth of modern cities. A land of sun-drenched landscapes, rich cultural heritage, and a people striving to build a brighter tomorrow, rooted in a past that continues to inspire awe. Egypt is not merely a location, but a story unfolding, a legacy being written with each new generation.

JavaScript is a versatile scripting language, a cornerstone of modern web development. Though possessing a relatively unassuming core, it’s a workhorse, breathing life into static webpages and powering dynamic interactions. It’s a language frequently found in the toolkit of designers and developers like Nagwa Ibrahim, who skillfully employed it – alongside HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, and jQuery – to craft a portfolio of websites including nitka.com, and several administrative interfaces for Nagpur United Corporation, Mintmetrix.com, Tagline Videos, Smartbadge, shreekiaspack.co.in, 3staragroproducts.com, and luckystationery.co.in. While Ibrahim also demonstrates foundational knowledge in languages like C, JAVA, and C++, JavaScript clearly occupies a central role in her web-focused skillset, enabling her to build functional and engaging online experiences. It’s a language that empowers creators to move beyond simple presentation, adding responsiveness and user-centric features to the digital landscape.

Adobe Illustrator is a vector graphics editor – a digital workshop for crafting scalable designs that remain crisp at any size. It’s a precision instrument favored by professionals and aspiring artists alike, capable of bringing logos, illustrations, typography, and complex artwork to life. Unlike pixel-based programs, Illustrator builds images using mathematical equations, meaning artwork doesn’t lose quality when enlarged. 

It’s a versatile tool, equally at home creating delicate icons for a website or bold graphics for a billboard. Illustrator’s power lies in its intricate pen tools, shape builders, and gradient capabilities, allowing for nuanced control over every line and curve. It seamlessly integrates with other Adobe products, forming a cornerstone of many creative workflows. 

Individuals like Nagwa Ibrahim, a Web and Graphics Designer at Virtuous Media Point, rely on Illustrator’s capabilities to produce professional-grade visual assets. Its proficiency is a key skill for designers seeking to translate ideas into visually compelling realities, standing alongside tools like Adobe Photoshop and Corel Draw in the arsenal of the modern graphic artist. It’s a program defined by its precision, scalability, and the boundless creativity it unlocks.

CSS3 is a versatile styling language, a core component in the digital toolkit of a modern web artisan like Nagwa Ibrahim. It’s not a standalone entity, but a powerful instrument wielded to breathe life and visual appeal into the skeletal structure of websites built with HTML5. Nagwa, throughout her work at Virtuous Media Point and during her internship at e-sense IT Solutions, consistently employed CSS3 – alongside companions like Bootstrap, JavaScript, and jQuery – to craft compelling online experiences. 

It’s a language of meticulous detail, allowing for precise control over layout, typography, color, and animation. Nagwa demonstrated this control in a diverse portfolio of projects, from the full design of sites like www.nitka.com and www.shreekiaspack.co.in, to administrative roles shaping the look and feel of Nagpur United Corporation, Mintmetrix.com, Tagline Videos, Smartbadge, and www.3staragroproducts.com, and www.luckystationery.co.in. 

While Nagwa’s foundational skills extend to languages like C, JAVA, and C++, CSS3 represents her focused expertise in the realm of front-end development – the art of shaping what the user *sees* and *experiences*. It’s a skill she honed through practical application, transforming requirements into visually engaging and functional web solutions. CSS3, in Nagwa’s hands, isn’t merely code; it’s a means of visual storytelling.

Bootstrap is a versatile and practical toolkit, favored by Nagwa Ibrahim, a web designer with a growing portfolio. It’s the foundation upon which she’s built numerous online presences – from the sleek designs of Shreekiaspack.co.in and 3staragroproducts.com to the administrative interfaces of Nagpur United Corporation and Smartbadge. Not a flashy, standalone creation, Bootstrap is a quiet enabler, a framework that allows Nagwa to rapidly translate ideas into functional, visually appealing websites. Alongside HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and jQuery, it forms a core part of her skillset, demonstrating a preference for efficient, front-end development. While proficient in more complex languages like C++ and Java, Nagwa consistently returns to Bootstrap as a reliable partner in bringing web projects to life, showcasing its utility in both professional roles at Virtuous Media Point and e-sense IT Solutions, and in her independent work. It’s a tool that speaks to her pragmatic approach – a focus on delivering effective, user-friendly web experiences.

Corel Draw is a versatile and powerful vector graphics editor, a digital artist’s workbench favored for its precision and flexibility. It’s a tool that empowers creators – like Nagwa Ibrahim, a Web and Graphics Designer at Virtuous Media Point – to bring visual concepts to life. While adept with industry standards like Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator, Nagwa demonstrably relies on Corel Draw’s capabilities for crafting designs, suggesting a preference for its unique approach to vector illustration. It’s not merely software; it’s a key component in a designer’s toolkit, enabling the creation of everything from intricate logos and illustrations to detailed website graphics. Corel Draw stands as a testament to the enduring need for precise, scalable graphics in the digital landscape, a silent partner in the work of professionals shaping the visual world.

Adobe Photoshop is the industry-standard raster graphics editor, a digital darkroom and artist’s canvas rolled into one powerful package. It’s a tool of meticulous detail and boundless creative possibility, capable of transforming photographs from simple snapshots into compelling visual stories, and birthing original artwork from a blank slate. Photoshop isn’t merely software; it’s a versatile partner for anyone shaping visual communication. 

For professionals like Nagwa Ibrahim, a Web and Graphics Designer at Virtuous Media Point, Photoshop was a key instrument in her toolkit – alongside Corel Draw and Illustrator – used to craft compelling graphic assets. It’s a program favored by those who demand precision, offering layer-based editing that allows for non-destructive manipulation of images. Whether refining colors, removing imperfections, composing complex designs, or creating photorealistic mockups, Photoshop provides the control and flexibility to realize almost any visual concept. Its proficiency is a hallmark of skilled designers, a testament to their ability to translate ideas into impactful imagery.

jQuery is a fast, small, and feature-rich JavaScript library. It simplifies HTML document traversing and manipulation, event handling, animation, and Ajax – essentially, the core tasks of modern web development. Though not a programming language itself, jQuery acts as a powerful toolkit, allowing developers to achieve more with less code. It’s a versatile component, frequently employed alongside foundational web technologies like HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript, and often integrated with frameworks like Bootstrap to streamline website creation. 

Nagwa Ibrahim, a web and graphics designer with experience at Virtuous Media Point and e-sense IT Solutions, frequently utilizes jQuery in her work. She’s demonstrably proficient in its application, having leveraged it in the development of numerous websites – including nitka.com, Nagpur United Corporation, Mintmetrix.com, and several others – showcasing a practical understanding of its capabilities. While possessing foundational skills in languages like C, JAVA, and C++, jQuery remains a key instrument in her web development skillset, enabling efficient and dynamic website functionality. It’s a tool she’s clearly comfortable with, forming a core part of her professional toolkit.

HTML5 is a foundational technology, a versatile toolkit in the hands of digital creators like Nagwa Ibrahim. It’s not a flashy newcomer, but the bedrock upon which modern web experiences are built – a standard for structuring content for the internet. While comfortable with more complex languages like C++, Java, and C, Ibrahim consistently returns to HTML5 as a core component of her work. 

She wields it with practiced ease, demonstrated through the numerous websites she’s brought to life: from the vibrant storefronts of Shreekiaspack and 3Star Agro Products, to the informational hubs of Nagpur United Corporation and Lucky Stationery.  Her portfolio, including projects like Nitka.com, Mintmetrix.com, Tagline Videos, and Smartbadge, showcases HTML5’s adaptability, often paired with CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and jQuery to deliver dynamic and visually appealing results. 

During her time at Virtuous Media Point and her internship at e-sense IT Solutions, HTML5 wasn’t merely a tool, but a constant companion in the process of translating ideas into functional, user-friendly websites. It’s a skill she honed through both professional experience and independent projects, a reliable foundation for building a digital presence.  Essentially, HTML5 is the silent architect of the web, and Ibrahim is a skilled builder who understands its language fluently.

Nagwa Ibrahim is a versatile Web and Graphics Designer, a digital architect with a keen eye for both form and function. Her foundation stems from a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Cairo University, built upon a solid academic record from the Egyptian Ministry of Education. From the outset, Nagwa demonstrated a practical bent, gaining early experience as a Web Designing and Development Intern at e-sense IT Solutions Pvt. Ltd. – a formative period where she participated in the full Software Development Life Cycle and honed her skills in gathering requirements and crafting website solutions. 

She transitioned into a full-time role at Virtuous Media Point, spending two years designing websites and compelling graphic assets. Nagwa isn’t confined to a single medium; she’s equally adept at crafting digital experiences and tangible visual communications. Her portfolio boasts a diverse range of projects, from complete website builds – including www.nitka.com, and sites for Nagpur United Corporation, Mintmetrix, Tagline Videos, Smartbadge, Shreekiaspak, 3Star Agroproducts, and Luckystationery – to impactful graphic designs like logos, brochures, advertising banners, and even large-scale hoardings. 

Technically proficient, Nagwa commands a suite of industry-standard tools, including HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, jQuery, Corel Draw, Adobe Photoshop, and Adobe Illustrator.  She can be reached via email at NAGWA.IBRAHIM@EMAIL.COM, by phone at +20 100 123 4567, and her work and profile can be found online at GITHUB.COM/NAGWA-IBRAHIM, LINKEDIN.COM/IN/NAGWA-IBRAHIM, and NAGWAIBRAHIM.COM.
